title: Gypsocallis tiariflora
taxonomy: Gypsocallis tiariflora is a flowering plant, and its classification includes being a member of the family Rutaceae, which comprises of plants commonly known as citrus or rue family. It is native to Western Australia. The genus Gypsocallis belongs to the tribe Boronieae. intro: Gypsocallis tiariflora, also known as Tiaré, is a tropical plant native to the islands of French Polynesia, characterized by its fragrant white flowers and rich essential oil. The essential oil extracted from Tiaré flowers has been traditionally used to promote skin and hair health, with benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help soothe skin irritations and reduce the appearance of acne and eczema. The bioactive constituents of Tiaré essential oil include geraniol, linalool, and benzaldehyde, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Tiaré essential oil is commonly used in aromatherapy and skincare products, such as soaps, creams, and shampoos, to promote relaxation and well-being.
